name: easykube

metadata.name: easykube
metadata.summary: Simple, asynchronous Python client for Kubernetes.

documentation:
# easykube

A simple, asynchronous Python client for Kubernetes.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.errors.ApiError
documentation: Exception that is raised when a Kubernetes API error occurs that is in the 4xx range.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.api
documentation: Returns an API object for the specified API version.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.api_preferred_version
documentation: Returns an API object for the given group at the preferred version.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient._resource_for_object
documentation: Returns a resource for the given object.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.create_object
documentation: Create the given object.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.replace_object
documentation: Replace the given object with the specified state.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.patch_object
documentation: Apply the given patch to the specified object.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.delete_object
documentation: Delete the specified object.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.apply_object
documentation:
Applies the given object using server-side apply.

See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/server-side-apply/.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.BaseClient.client_side_apply_object
documentation: Create or update the given object, equivalent to "kubectl apply".
Base class for sync and async clients.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.SyncClient
documentation: Sync client for Kubernetes.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.client.AsyncClient
documentation: Async client for Kubernetes.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.spec.ResourceSpec

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.spec.ResourceSpec.__call__
documentation: Return a resource instance for the given client.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.spec.ResourceSpec.from_crd
documentation: Returns a resource spec for the given CRD definition.
Specification for a Kubernetes API resource.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api.get_flow_executor
documentation: Returns the flow executor to use.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api.api_version
documentation: Returns the API version for the API.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api._ensure_resources
documentation: Ensures that the resources have been loaded.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api.resources
documentation: Returns the resources for the API.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.api.Api.resource
documentation:
Returns a resource for the given name.

The given name can be either the plural name, the singular name or the kind.
Lookups by plural name will be faster as that is the key that is indexed.
Class for a Kubernetes API.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.iterators.ListResponseIterator

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.iterators.ListResponseIterator.resource_version
documentation: The last seen resource version.
Iterator for list responses.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.iterators.WatchEvents
documentation: Stream iterator that yields watch events.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.DeletePropagationPolicy
documentation: Enumeration of possible delete policies.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.json_patch
documentation: Patches the specified instance with the given data, treated as a JSON Patch.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.json_merge_patch
documentation: Patches the specified instance with the given data, treated as a JSON Patch.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.server_side_apply
documentation:
Uses server-side apply to create or update the specified object.

See https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/using-api/server-side-apply/.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.delete_all
documentation: Deletes a collection of resources.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.watch_list
documentation:
Watches a set of resource instances, as specified by the given parameters, for changes.

Returns a tuple of (initial state, watch events).

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.client.resource.Resource.watch_one
documentation:
Watches a single resource instance for changes.

Returns a tuple of (initial state, async iterator of watch events).
Class for Kubernetes REST resources.

functions.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.cleanup_tempfile
documentation: Cleans up the specified file in a robust way.

functions.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.file_or_data
documentation: Returns the path to a file containing the data for the specified key.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.ConfigurationError
documentation: Raised when there is an error with the configuration.

classes.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.async_client
documentation: Returns a new asynchronous client using the configuration.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.sync_client
documentation: Returns a new synchronous client using the configuration.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.from_kubeconfig_data
documentation: Return a configuration for the given kubeconfig data, which can be bytes or str.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.from_kubeconfig
documentation: Return a configuration for the given kubeconfig file.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.from_serviceaccount
documentation: Return a configuration for the configured service account.

methods.name: easykube.kubernetes.config.Configuration.from_environment
documentation: Return a configuration from available information in the environment.
Configuration object that produces clients.

classes.name: easykube.rest.util.PropertyDict
documentation: Dictionary that also supports property access.

classes.name: easykube.rest.client.BaseClient

methods.name: easykube.rest.client.BaseClient.request
documentation: Builds and sends a request, respecting any custom JSON encoder.

methods.name: easykube.rest.client.BaseClient.send
documentation: Sends the given request as part of a flow.

methods.name: easykube.rest.client.BaseClient.raise_for_status
documentation: Raise the relevant exception for the response, if required.

methods.name: easykube.rest.client.BaseClient.delete
documentation:
Sends a delete request.

We override the delete from HTTPX in order to be able to send a request body.
Base class for sync and async REST clients.

classes.name: easykube.rest.client.SyncClient
documentation: Class for a sync REST client.

classes.name: easykube.rest.client.AsyncClient
documentation: Class for a REST client.

functions.name: easykube.flow.flow
documentation:
Decorator that marks a method of a flowable as a flow that can be resolved synchronously
or asynchronously depending on the executor that is in use.

classes.name: easykube.flow.SyncExecutor

methods.name: easykube.flow.SyncExecutor.execute_flow
documentation: Executes a flow and returns the result.
Flow executor for synchronous contexts.

classes.name: easykube.flow.AsyncExecutor

methods.name: easykube.flow.AsyncExecutor.execute_flow
documentation: Executes a flow and returns an awaitable containing the result.
Flow executor for asynchronous contexts.

classes.name: easykube.flow.Flowable
documentation: Base classes for objects that can support flows.

classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator.get_flow_executor
documentation: Returns the flow executor to use.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator._extract_list
documentation:
Extracts a list of instances from a list response.

By default, this is deferred to the resource method so cases where a custom iterator
is required are rare.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator._extract_next_page
documentation:
Return a (URL, params) tuple for the next page or None if there is no next page.

By default, this is deferred to the resource method so cases where a custom iterator
is required are rare.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator._next_item
documentation: Flow that returns the next item in the iterator.

inner_classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ListResponseIterator.StopIteration
documentation: Exception raised to indicate that a list response has finished iterating.

Iterator for list responses.

Can be used as either a sync or async iterator depending on the client that is given.

classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._request
documentation: Returns the request for the iterator.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._send
documentation:
Context manager that synchronously sends a request and yields a response, ensuring
that the current response is updated.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._send_async
documentation:
Context manager that asynchronously sends a request and yields a response, ensuring
that the current response is updated.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns a synchronous chunk iterator for the response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._async_chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns an asynchronous chunk iterator for the response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._process_chunk
documentation:
Process the given chunk of bytes and return the item to be yielded.

To suppress the yielding of an item for a chunk, raise the special exception
StreamIterator.SuppressItem.

By default, chunks are processed as JSON.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator._should_resume
documentation:
Receives the exception that caused the stream to exit, or None if the stream
terminated without error, and returns a truthy value if the stream should be
resumed.

inner_classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.StreamIterator.SuppressItem
documentation: Exception that can be raised to suppress an item from being yielded.

Iterator over items in a stream.

Can be used as a sync or async iterator depending on the client that is given.

classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ByteStreamIterator

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ByteStreamIterator._chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns a synchronous chunk iterator for the response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.ByteStreamIterator._async_chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns an asynchronous chunk iterator for the response.

Iterator for items in a byte stream.

Chunks correspond to fixed-size chunks of a byte-stream, and are received by
_process_chunk as bytes.

classes.name: easykube.rest.iterators.TextStreamIterator

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.TextStreamIterator._chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns a synchronous chunk iterator for the response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.iterators.TextStreamIterator._async_chunk_iterator
documentation: Returns an asynchronous chunk iterator for the response.

Iterator for items in a text stream.

Chunks correspond to lines of the response.

classes.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.get_flow_executor
documentation: Returns the flow executor to use.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._ensure_initialised
documentation:
Ensures that the resource is fully initialised including any HTTP operations.

This will be called multiple times, so should become a no-op in the case where
the resource is already initialised.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._prepare_path
documentation:
Prepare the path to use for the given parameters.

Returns a (path, params) tuple where the path parameters have interpolated
and the remaining params should be given as URL parameters.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._prepare_data
documentation: Prepare data for submitting as part of a create, replace or patch request.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._extract_list
documentation: Extracts a list of instances from a list response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._extract_next_page
documentation:
Given a list response, return a (URL, params) tuple for the next page or None
if there is no next page.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._extract_one
documentation: Extracts a single instance from a fetch, create or update response.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._wrap_instance
documentation: Receives an instance and wraps the instance if required.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.list
documentation: Returns an iterable of the resource instances that match the given parameters.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.first
documentation:
Returns the first instance of a resource that matches the parameters, or None if one
does not exist.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.create
documentation: Creates an instance of the resource and returns it.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.fetch
documentation: Returns the data for the specified instance.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.replace
documentation: Replaces the specified instance with the given data.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.patch
documentation: Patches the specified instance with the given data.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource._create_or_update
documentation:
Flow that attempts to update an instance using the given data and method. If the
instance does not exist, it is created with the given data.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.create_or_replace
documentation:
Attempts to replace the specified instance of the resource with the given data.
If it does not exist, a new instance is created with the given data instead.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.create_or_patch
documentation:
Attempts to patch the specified instance of the resource with the given data.
If it does not exist, a new instance is created with the given data instead.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.delete
documentation: Delete the specified instance.

methods.name: easykube.rest.resource.Resource.action
documentation:
Executes an "action" for the specified instance.

Executing an action means making a POST request to `/<resourcepath>/<id>/<action>`.
Class for a REST resource.
